## Current State
Reopen has partial E2E coverage. Needs expansion.

## Commands to Test
- `br reopen <id>` - Reopen closed issue
- `br reopen <id> --reason <text>` - Reopen with reason
- `br reopen --json` - JSON output

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Reopen closed issue → status open
2. Reopen with reason, verify in notes/history
3. Reopen deferred issue (undefer)
4. JSON output structure

### Error Cases
5. Reopen already-open issue → error or no-op
6. Reopen non-existent issue → error
7. Reopen tombstone → error or allowed?

### Edge Cases
8. Reopen then close again (cycle)
9. Reopen issue with dependencies still open
10. Reopen preserves all other fields
11. Reopen updates updated_at timestamp

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_reopen.rs (or extend existing)

 {"id":"beads_rust-gpq","title":"Create README.md with quick-start guide","description":"# README.md Creation

## Purpose
Create a comprehensive README.md that serves as the project landing page, enabling users to understand and start using br within 30 seconds.

## Structure

### Header Section
```markdown
# br - Beads Rust 🦀

A fast, non-invasive issue tracker for git repositories. Rust port of [beads](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads).

[\![CI](https://github.com/.../actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](...)
[\![Crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/beads-rust.svg)](...)
[\![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](...)
```

### Quick Install
```markdown
## Quick Install

\`\`\`bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh | bash
\`\`\`

Or with Homebrew:
\`\`\`bash
brew install dicklesworthstone/tap/br
\`\`\`
```

### TL;DR
```markdown
## TL;DR

br is a local-first issue tracker that stores issues in SQLite with JSONL export for git-based collaboration. It's designed to be non-invasive: no daemons, no git hooks, no auto-commits.

\`\`\`bash
br init                              # Initialize in current repo
br create "Fix login bug" -p 1       # Create high-priority bug
br list                              # Show all issues
br ready                             # Show actionable work
br close bd-abc123                   # Close an issue
br sync --flush-only                 # Export to JSONL for git
\`\`\`
```

### Features Table
```markdown
## Features

| Feature | Status | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| Issue CRUD | ✅ | Create, read, update, delete issues |
| Dependencies | ✅ | Block/unblock relationships |
| Labels | ✅ | Categorize with custom labels |
| Search | ✅ | Full-text search across issues |
| JSONL Sync | ✅ | Git-friendly export/import |
| AI Agent Mode | 🚧 | Structured output for AI tools |
| TUI Mode | 📋 | Interactive terminal UI |
```

### AI Agent Integration
```markdown
## AI Agent Integration

br is designed to work seamlessly with AI coding agents like Claude Code:

\`\`\`bash
# Get machine-readable help
br --robot-help

# Get prioritized work for agent
br --robot-triage

# Structured JSON output
br list --json
\`\`\`

See [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) for complete agent integration guide.
```

### Quick Example
```markdown
## Quick Example

\`\`\`bash
# Initialize br in your project
cd my-project
br init

# Create your first issue
br create --title "Implement user authentication" --type feature --priority 1

# Add a dependency
br create --title "Set up database schema" --type task
br dep add bd-xyz bd-abc  # xyz depends on abc

# See what's ready to work on
br ready

# Claim work
br update bd-abc --status in_progress

# Complete and sync
br close bd-abc --reason "Schema implemented"
br sync --flush-only
git add .beads/ && git commit -m "Update issues"
\`\`\`
```

### Architecture
```markdown
## Architecture

\`\`\`
┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
│   CLI (br)  │────▶│ SQLite Store │────▶│ JSONL Sync  │
└─────────────┘     └──────────────┘     └─────────────┘
                           │                    │
                           ▼                    ▼
                    .beads/beads.db      .beads/issues.jsonl
\`\`\`

- **SQLite**: Primary storage, WAL mode, concurrent access
- **JSONL**: Git-friendly export for collaboration
- **No daemon**: Simple CLI, no background processes
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README.md exists at project root
- [ ] Installation instructions are correct
- [ ] Quick example works as written
- [ ] All links are valid
- [ ] Badges display correctly
- [ ] Mobile-friendly (readable on GitHub mobile)

## Dependencies
- Installation script (for accurate install commands)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenPuma","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:51:07.2578799Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T22:54:31.424817281Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T22:54:31.424817281Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive README.md with quick-start guide, features table, AI agent integration, architecture diagram, safety model reference, and full command reference. Fixed LICENSE link. All other links verified."}
-{"id":"beads_rust-gs0","title":"Phase 5: Polish & Conformance - Production Readiness","description":"# Phase 5: Polish & Conformance

## Goals
Deliver a production-ready `br` with **classic bd parity**, comprehensive testing, and ergonomic utility commands that do not expand scope beyond the three planning docs.

## Deliverables (children)
### Utility/Inspection Commands
- `doctor` (read-only diagnostics; no auto-fixes)
- `info`, `where`, `version`
- `stats/status`, `count`, `stale`, `orphans`
- `defer` / `undefer`
- `epic status` + `close-eligible`
- `graph` (deps visualization)
- `audit` (interactions.jsonl)
- `history` (.br_history) + `changelog`
- `q` (quick capture)
- `lint` (template/config validation)
- `saved queries` (save/run/list/delete)
- CSV export for list/export

### Test & QA Infrastructure
- Unit test infra and specs
- Snapshot/golden tests for human output
- E2E integration tests (CLI workflows)
- Conformance harness (bd vs br JSON parity)
- CI pipeline (fmt + clippy + tests)

### UX & Performance Polish
- Color output (opt-in or auto)
- Progress indicators for long ops
- Shell completions
- Performance benchmarks (startup + common paths)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conformance suite green; JSON parity with bd for classic commands.
- Utility commands are stable, documented, and tested.
- CI runs `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, and all tests.
- Performance baselines recorded; regressions detectable.

## Notes
- No daemon, no git hooks, no auto-git behavior.
- Utility commands must be read-only unless explicitly documented.

 {"id":"beads_rust-gu7b","title":"Code coverage reporting with tarpaulin","description":"# Code Coverage Reporting

## Overview
Configure cargo-tarpaulin for coverage reporting and CI integration.

## Installation
```bash
cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
```

## Configuration

### tarpaulin.toml
```toml
[coverage]
ignore-tests = true
follow-exec = true
timeout = "300s"
out = ["Html", "Lcov", "Json"]
output-dir = "coverage"

[coverage.run]
jobs = 4

[coverage.exclude]
# Exclude benchmarks and examples
paths = ["benches/*", "examples/*"]

# Exclude test helpers
test-patterns = ["test_helpers::*"]
```

## Coverage Targets

### Module Coverage Goals
| Module | Target | Priority |
|--------|--------|----------|
| src/storage/ | 90%+ | Critical - core data layer |
| src/sync/ | 85%+ | Critical - data integrity |
| src/cli/commands/ | 80%+ | High - user-facing |
| src/model/ | 85%+ | High - domain logic |
| src/format/ | 75%+ | Medium - output formatting |
| src/util/ | 70%+ | Medium - helpers |

### Overall Goals
- Minimum threshold: 70%
- Target: 85%+
- Stretch: 90%+

## Logging Requirements

### Coverage Run Logging
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# coverage.sh - Run with logging

echo "coverage_run_start: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo "coverage_config: ignore_tests=true output_dir=coverage"

# Run tarpaulin with verbose output
cargo tarpaulin --out Html --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage/tarpaulin.log

# Parse and log results
COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
LINES_COVERED=$(jq '.covered_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
TOTAL_LINES=$(jq '.total_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)

echo "coverage_result: percent=${COVERAGE} covered=${LINES_COVERED} total=${TOTAL_LINES}"

# Per-module breakdown
echo "coverage_module_breakdown:"
jq -r '.files[] | "  \(.path): \(.coverage)%"' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json

echo "coverage_run_end: $(date -Iseconds)"
```

### CI Integration Logging
```yaml
# .github/workflows/coverage.yml
- name: Generate coverage
  run: |
    echo "::group::Coverage Generation"
    cargo tarpaulin --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage.log
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    # Log summary
    COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' tarpaulin-report.json)
    echo "coverage_summary: ${COVERAGE}%"
    echo "::set-output name=coverage::${COVERAGE}"
    
    # Fail if below threshold
    if (( $(echo "$COVERAGE < 70" | bc -l) )); then
      echo "::error::Coverage ${COVERAGE}% below minimum 70%"
      exit 1
    fi

- name: Upload to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
    verbose: true
```

### Per-Module Coverage Logging
```rust
// In test harness, log coverage per module
#[cfg(test)]
mod coverage_logging {
    use std::process::Command;
    
    fn log_module_coverage(module: &str) {
        let output = Command::new("cargo")
            .args(["tarpaulin", "--out", "Json", "--packages", module])
            .output()
            .expect("tarpaulin failed");
        
        if output.status.success() {
            let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
            let coverage = json["coverage_percent"].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
            println!("coverage_module: {}={:.1}%", module, coverage);
        }
    }
}
```

## Usage
```bash
# Generate coverage report
cargo tarpaulin --out Html

# View report
open coverage/tarpaulin-report.html

# Quick coverage check
cargo tarpaulin --out Stdout --skip-clean

# Module-specific coverage
cargo tarpaulin --packages beads_rust --out Html -- --test storage
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tarpaulin.toml configured with proper exclusions
- [ ] Coverage report generates locally
- [ ] CI workflow for coverage with threshold check
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Per-module breakdown logging
- [ ] Minimum 70% coverage enforced
- [ ] HTML + Lcov + JSON outputs generated

## Commands to Test
- `br comments add <issue_id> <text>` - Add comment to issue
- `br comments list <issue_id>` - List comments on issue
- `br comments --json` - JSON output mode

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Add single comment, verify in list
2. Add multiple comments, verify order (newest last)
3. List comments with --json, validate structure
4. Add comment to issue with existing comments

### Error Cases
5. Add comment to non-existent issue → error
6. Add empty comment → error or rejection
7. List comments on issue with no comments → empty list

### Edge Cases
8. Comment with special characters (quotes, newlines, unicode)
9. Very long comment (near limits)
10. Comment on closed issue (should work)

## Test Infrastructure
- Use tests/common/cli.rs BrWorkspace
- Capture stdout/stderr with logging
- Assert JSON shapes match expected
- Verify comment persists in DB and JSONL

 {"id":"beads_rust-h2c","title":"Audit events: insertion rules + retrieval ordering","description":"# Audit Events (events table)

## Purpose
Implement the audit/event model exactly as classic bd: every mutation writes an event row inside the same transaction, and events are **local DB only** (never exported to JSONL). This bead defines event schema, insertion rules, and retrieval ordering.

## Schema (SQLite)
- `events` table: `id` (autoinc), `issue_id`, `event_type`, `actor`, `old_value`, `new_value`, `comment`, `created_at`.
- Indexes: `events.issue_id`, `events.event_type`, `events.created_at`, `events.actor` (see schema bead).

## Event Types (classic)
- `created`
- `updated`
- `status_changed`
- `closed`
- `reopened`
- `commented`
- `dependency_added` / `dependency_removed`
- `label_added` / `label_removed`
- `deleted` (tombstone) / `restored` (if restore supported)
- `compacted` (schema includes compaction fields even if compaction excluded)

## Insertion Rules (must be atomic with mutation)
- **CreateIssue**: emit `created` with actor.
- **UpdateIssue**: emit:
  - `status_changed` for non-terminal status transitions.
  - `closed` when status becomes `closed`.
  - `reopened` when moving from `closed` to `open`.
  - `updated` for other field changes.
- **CloseIssue**: `closed` with `comment` = close reason.
- **ReopenIssue**: `reopened` (and optionally a comment if `--reason` adds a comment).
- **DeleteIssue**: `deleted` with delete reason.
- **RestoreIssue** (if supported): `restored`.
- **Dep add/remove**: `dependency_added` / `dependency_removed` with comment like:
  - `Added dependency: <issue> <type> <depends_on>`
  - `Removed dependency on <depends_on>`
- **Label add/remove**: `label_added` / `label_removed`.
- **Comment add**: `commented` with `comment` text.

## Retrieval Semantics
- `GetEvents(issue_id, limit)` returns **newest first** (created_at DESC).
- Events are **not** exported to JSONL.
- CLI should only expose events via `show --events` (if implemented) or similar.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every mutation path inserts exactly one appropriate event row.
- Events are created **within the same transaction** as the mutation + dirty mark.
- `GetEvents` ordering is `created_at DESC`.
- JSONL export/import ignores events.

## Tests
- Unit tests for each mutation path verifying event_type + actor + timestamps.
- Ordering test for `GetEvents` (DESC).
- Integration test: create/update/close/dep/label/comment produce expected event sequence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:05:22.134434351Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:47:59.406508805Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:47:59.406508805Z","close_reason":"Implemented audit events module: schema, insertion functions, GetEvents with DESC ordering, 13 unit tests passing"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-hvf","title":"show Command Implementation","description":"# show Command

## Purpose
Display full issue details (including labels, dependencies, dependents, comments) with classic output format.

## CLI
```
br show <id...> [OPTIONS]
br show [OPTIONS]  # Uses last-touched if no ID
```

## Flags
- `<id...>`: One or more issue IDs (partial resolution supported).
- `--short`: Compact single-line output.
- `--deps`: Include dependency details.
- `--comments`: Include comments.
- `--events`: Include event history.
- `--refs`: Include external references.
- `--json`: JSON output.
- `--robot`: Machine-readable output (alias for --json).

## Behavior
1. Resolve ID(s) via partial matching.
2. Set last-touched to the first shown ID.
3. Fetch full issue details including:
   - All issue fields.
   - Labels (always included).
   - Dependencies (blocking deps).
   - Dependents (issues this blocks).
   - Comments (if `--comments` or full view).
   - Events (if `--events`).
   - Parent issue (if exists).
4. JSON output is ALWAYS an array (even for single ID).

## Output

### JSON (IssueDetails schema)
```json
[
  {
    "id": "bd-abc12",
    "title": "Implement feature X",
    "description": "Full description here...",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "feature",
    "assignee": "alice",
    "created_at": "2025-01-10T09:00:00Z",
    "labels": ["backend", "api"],
    "dependencies": [
      {"depends_on_id": "bd-xyz89", "type": "blocks"}
    ],
    "dependents": [
      {"issue_id": "bd-def34", "type": "blocks"}
    ],
    "comments": [
      {"id": 1, "author": "bob", "text": "LGTM", "created_at": "2025-01-11T10:00:00Z"}
    ],
    "parent": "bd-epic1"
  }
]
```

### Text Output (Full View)
```
bd-abc12: Implement feature X
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Status: open          Priority: P1
Type: feature         Assignee: alice
Created: 2025-01-10   Owner: team-lead

Description:
  Full description here explaining what this
  feature should do and why.

Design:
  Technical design notes...

Acceptance Criteria:
  - [ ] Criterion 1
  - [ ] Criterion 2

Labels: backend, api

Dependencies (2):
  • bd-xyz89: Database schema [P0] [open]
  • bd-qrs12: API design [P1] [in_progress]

Blocking (1):
  • bd-def34: Write tests [P2] [blocked]

Comments (1):
  bob (2025-01-11):
    LGTM
```

### Short Output (`--short`)
```
bd-abc12 [P1] [open] Implement feature X (alice) [backend, api]
```

## Data Structures

### IssueDetails
```rust
pub struct IssueDetails {
    #[serde(flatten)]
    pub issue: Issue,
    pub labels: Vec<String>,
    pub dependencies: Vec<DependencyInfo>,
    pub dependents: Vec<DependencyInfo>,
    pub comments: Vec<Comment>,
    pub parent: Option<String>,
}

pub struct DependencyInfo {
    pub issue_id: String,
    pub depends_on_id: String,
    pub dep_type: String,
    pub title: Option<String>,  // For display
    pub status: Option<String>, // For display
}
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: ID does not resolve → error with suggestions.
- **AmbiguousId**: ID resolves to multiple → error with candidate list.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(ids = ?ids, "Showing issue details");
tracing::debug!(id = %id, "Resolved issue");
tracing::debug!(labels = ?labels, deps = ?deps.len(), "Fetched relations");
tracing::trace!(comments = ?comments.len(), "Loaded comments");
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON array output matches bd.
- Text output matches golden snapshots.
- IssueDetails includes all relation types.
- Last-touched set correctly.

## Unit Tests (storage layer)
```rust
// tests/storage/show_tests.rs
test_get_issue_details_basic
test_get_issue_details_with_labels
test_get_issue_details_with_dependencies
test_get_issue_details_with_dependents

test_get_issue_details_with_parent
test_get_issue_details_not_found
test_get_issue_details_sets_last_touched

```

## Integration Tests (CLI)
```rust
// tests/integration/show_tests.rs
#[test]
fn test_show_basic() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Show test issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Show test issue"))
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains(&id));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_description() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "With description", "--description", "This is the full body"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let id = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("This is the full body"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_labels() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "Labeled issue", "--labels", "backend,api"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let id = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("backend"))
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("api"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_dependencies() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let blocker = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocking issue");
    let blocked = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Dependent issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "add", &blocked, &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Show blocked issue should list dependency
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &blocked])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains(&blocker).or(predicate::str::contains("Blocking issue")));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_dependents() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let blocker = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocking issue");
    let blocked = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Dependent issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "add", &blocked, &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Show blocker should list what it blocks
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains(&blocked).or(predicate::str::contains("Dependent")));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_json_output() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "JSON show test");
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(json.is_array());
    assert_eq!(json[0]["title"], "JSON show test");
    assert!(json[0]["labels"].is_array());
    assert!(json[0]["dependencies"].is_array());
    assert!(json[0]["dependents"].is_array());
}

#[test]
fn test_show_multiple_ids() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id1 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 1");
    let id2 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 2");
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id1, &id2, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(json.as_array().unwrap().len(), 2);
}

#[test]
fn test_show_short_format() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "Short format test", "--priority", "1", "--labels", "test"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let id = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id, "--short"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
    // Short format should be one line
    let lines: Vec<&str> = stdout.trim().lines().collect();
    assert_eq!(lines.len(), 1);
    assert!(stdout.contains(&id));
    assert!(stdout.contains("Short format test"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_not_found() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", "bd-nonexistent"])
        .assert()
        .failure()
        .stderr(predicate::str::contains("not found"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_partial_id() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "Partial ID test", "--id", "beads_rust-abc123"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Should resolve partial ID
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", "abc123"])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Partial ID test"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_last_touched() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Last touched test");
    
    // Show sets last-touched
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Show without ID uses last-touched
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .arg("show")
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Last touched test"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_with_parent() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let parent = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Parent epic");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "Child task", "--parent", &parent])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let child = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &child, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(json[0]["parent"].is_string() || json[0]["dependencies"].as_array().unwrap().iter().any(|d| d["type"] == "parent-child"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_includes_all_fields() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args([
            "create", "Complete issue",
            "--description", "Full description",
            "--design", "Design notes",
            "--acceptance", "Acceptance criteria",
            "--notes", "Additional notes",
            "--priority", "1",
            "--type", "feature",
            "--assignee", "alice",
            "--labels", "backend,api"
        ])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let id = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    let issue = &json[0];
    
    assert_eq!(issue["title"], "Complete issue");
    assert_eq!(issue["description"], "Full description");
    assert_eq!(issue["design"], "Design notes");
    assert_eq!(issue["acceptance_criteria"], "Acceptance criteria");
    assert_eq!(issue["notes"], "Additional notes");
    assert_eq!(issue["priority"], 1);
    assert_eq!(issue["issue_type"], "feature");
    assert_eq!(issue["assignee"], "alice");
}
```

## Conformance Tests
```rust
// tests/conformance/show_tests.rs
conformance_test! {
    name: "show_basic",
    setup: ["create Show test issue"],
    br_command: "br show <id1> --json",
    bd_command: "bd show <id1> --json",
    compare: ContainsFields(vec!["id", "title", "status", "labels", "dependencies"]),
}

conformance_test! {
    name: "show_with_relations",
    setup: [
        "create Blocker",
        "create Blocked",
        "dep add <id2> <id1>",
    ],
    br_command: "br show <id2> --json",
    bd_command: "bd show <id2> --json",
    compare: ContainsFields(vec!["id", "dependencies"]),
}

conformance_test! {
    name: "show_multiple",
    setup: ["create Issue 1", "create Issue 2"],
    br_command: "br show <id1> <id2> --json",
    bd_command: "bd show <id1> <id2> --json",
    compare: ArrayLength(2),
}

conformance_test! {
    name: "show_short",
    setup: ["create Short test"],
    br_command: "br show <id1> --short",
    bd_command: "bd show <id1> --short",
    compare: SingleLine,
}
```

 {"id":"beads_rust-i7s","title":"where Command Implementation","description":"# where Command Implementation

## Purpose
Report the active `.beads` directory location, including redirects and prefix detection.

## Behavior
- Find active beads dir (respect `BEADS_DIR` if set).
- If `.beads/redirect` exists, follow it and report `redirected_from`.
- Detect prefix:
  - Prefer DB config `issue_prefix` if DB available.
  - Else parse prefix from first JSONL line ID.
- Error when no beads dir:
  - Text: error + hint, exit 1.
  - JSON: `{ "error": "no beads directory found" }`, exit 1.

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "path": "/abs/path/.beads",
  "redirected_from": "/abs/other/.beads",
  "prefix": "bd",
  "database_path": "/abs/path/.beads/beads.db"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct path resolution with and without redirect.
- Prefix detection matches classic order.
- Error behavior matches bd.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyOwl","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:32.897767057Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T06:00:03.997180494Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T06:00:03.997180494Z","close_reason":"Implemented where command with text and JSON output modes.  {"id":"beads_rust-j57","title":"audit Command (interactions.jsonl)","description":"# audit Command (interactions.jsonl)

## Purpose
Optional agent interaction logging to `.beads/interactions.jsonl`, matching bd audit schemas. Read/write only; no daemon required.

## CLI
```
br audit record [--kind ... --issue-id ... --model ... --prompt ... --response ... --tool-name ... --exit-code ... --error ... --stdin]
br audit label <entry-id> --label <label> [--reason <text>]
```

## Storage
- Append-only JSONL at `.beads/interactions.jsonl`.
- Entry IDs prefixed `int-` (random 4 bytes, hex).

## JSON Shapes
- `record` output: `{ "id": "int-...", "kind": "..." }`
- `label` output: `{ "id": "...", "parent_id": "...", "label": "..." }`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Appends valid JSONL entries with RFC3339 timestamps.
- Accepts stdin JSON when `--stdin` or no explicit fields.

## Tests
- Record + label output shapes.
- File append order preserved.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"opus_main","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:36.926687577Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T08:52:27.153121548Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T08:52:27.153077976Z","close_reason":"audit command fully implemented: record subcommand supports --kind, --issue-id, --model, --prompt, --response, --tool-name, --exit-code, --error, --stdin flags. label subcommand implemented. Appends to .beads/interactions.jsonl. JSON output enabled."}
